clean_text: Nephews. Alhamdulillah. Allah subhanahu waala mentions to us as we have been listening to in the past few days. He mentions to us the incident when chose disbelief over belief. chose to turn away from Allah subhanahu wa ta'ala over accepting and embracing the Quran the prophet sallallahu alaii wasallam and the worship of Allah subhanahu wa ta'ala alone without any partners the question here a lot of times many of us we have a kind of lingering emotion that is often incorrect. It's a misconception. It's a misconception that many people have and that is the idea that those who disbelieved in the prophet sallall.aii alaihi wasallam especially during his lifetime that they disbelieve with a sincere intention. Meaning they disbelieve sincerely. Disbelieve sincerely. What does that mean? Meaning that they hear the Quran, they hear the sunnah of the prophet sallall.aii wasallam. They meet the prophet Muhammad sallall.aii wasallam and they genuinely in their heart of hearts believe that he is not a prophet and the Quran is not from Allah subhanahu wa ta'ala in their heart of hearts and this is incorrect. Allah subhanahu wa ta'ala says We would not punish any people before first sending to them a messenger. Meaning before they have the ability to make a conscious decision that they know the truth but instead insist upon disbelief. this occurrence happened here. And what do people if if it's not truly confusion or sincerity that drives people to disbelieve, what is it then? What is the leading cause? If we were to really boil it down, what is the reason that the people of Quraysh other people d throughout the prophet sallallahu alaihi wasallam's lifetime, those who have met him, those who disbelieved, those who fought against him, if we were to really boil it down, what was the reason that they disbelieved? In so many words, they did not reject necessarily the Quran but rejected its implications. They did not necessarily reject the Sunnah or Muhammad Sall. Wasam, but they rejected the implications. And even today, if you have met people that have been perhaps on the fence about becoming Muslim and you they know about Islam, but they reject it or or refuse to become Muslim. If you were to really ask them and get to the bottom of it, you'll find that it's not really the Quran, it's not really Islam that they're rejecting per se. They're rejecting the implications that this religion will have on their lives because a shan hyperfocuses on implications when he scares people away from believing in Islam. What do we mean by implications? Meaning if I become Muslim for instance with what were the implications? He knew in his heart of hearts that Musa was a messenger. Allah tells us that he knew that that they outwardly rejected it. But Pharaoh outwardly rejected it. But in his heart of hearts, he knew that it was the truth. Why did this happen? Again, because of the implications. What were the implications? , he's not going to be Pharaoh anymore. He's not going to be the pharaoh. He's not going to be rich. He can't do what he wants to do. He's not going to be able to or he won't have. This is why people turn away. You look at Abu Jah. What was the reason he turned away? Were the implications? The implications were if I accept Islam, I have to become a follower to Beni Hashim tribalism. And no way will I ever be become a follower to Beni Hashim me and my tribe. You look at Abdullahb the leader and spearhead of the hypocrites in Medina. Allah subhanahu wa ta'ala tells us about throughout the chapters that were revealed in Medina in the Medini era. He is similar in many ways in his enmity and his antagonism of the prophet sallall.aii wasallam and his enmity towards Islam. He is very similar to Abu Jah. He he could he could even be looked at as a kind of Abu Jah of Medina. What was the reason that he rejected? Because the companions tell us Zabit and others they tell us why did he reject? Because he was the chieftain and the leader of the Khaz tribe in Medina. and he was considered the be all and end all chieftain of Medina. Anything that happened people when they wanted to get married, they'd go to Abdullahb Sal. He was wealthy. people had disputes. They'd come to him to settle their disputes. Any gathering he sat in, he was, you know, highly respected and and honored. And so what happened here? This caused him to reject Islam. How did it cause him to reject Islam? He was supposed to be the king of Medina. He was supposed to be the king of Medina to the point that some companions said just before the prophet sallall.aii wasallam arrived at Medina, he was almost going to be crowned as the king of Medina, the king of Muhammad sallall.aii alaihi wasallam came and in his mind ruined all of that. It wasn't because he didn't believe the Quran was the words of Allah. A person who is genuinely confused and has sincerity and is seeking with their all and every fiber of their being to be guided but is genuinely misunderstanding. This person is not eligible for hell. Hellfire is a place you have to choose to go to. It's a place that you have to make a conscious decision to go to. Paradise, no. There are people that enter paradise that never made that decision merely from the grace and mercy of Allah subhanahu wa ta'ala. But the hellfire, no. You have to use your own free will and have the knowledge of what you're rejecting and consciously choose to reject and oftent times choose over and over and over and over again to reject until Allah subhanahu wa ta'ala finally punishes a person with damnation as we see in the story of as we see in the story of the people of L the people of Hoot the people of the people of Muhammad sallallah.aii alaihi wasallam the chieftains of Quraysh these people were not punished immediately for 13 long years they made the decision over and over and over again to reject why were the the implications and those implications you'll hear them in people's speech even to this day as we mentioned if you meet someone who's knows about Islam and maybe even says good things about Islam You say, "Well, why don't you become Muslim?" Well, you know, my family wouldn't like that. It's the implications. Why don't you become Muslim? Well, you know, this this would be something that is that is upsetting to my wife. I don't want to upset my wife. My wife won't let me become Muslim. Right? I've actually had people tell me that before. This is the facial expression of someone who is taken over by his ego and arrogance. What came into his mind when he thought that? How could I How could I be a follower to this man? summary: This lecture discusses the true reasons why people reject Islam and faith, using Quranic examples and analysis. The speaker emphasizes that disbelief among the Quraysh and others was not due to sincere confusion or genuine misunderstanding, but rather due to the implications that accepting Islam would have on their lives—such as loss of power, wealth, status, and worldly pleasures. Examples include Pharaoh, Abu Jahl, and Abdullah ibn Ubayy, who rejected Islam to preserve their positions of authority and influence. The speaker explains that people today similarly reject Islam not because it is unclear, but because of lifestyle implications. The lecture then transitions to Quranic descriptions of Saqar (one of the names of hellfire), detailing its severity, the 70,000 ropes with 70,000 angels pulling it, and the 19 angels commissioned to oversee the torment of the damned.
suggested_title: The Real Reason People Reject Islam: Understanding the Implications of Faith
